reference: My dad had a basal ganglia stroke on August twenty-eight, two thousand and twelve. He can move his right arm and leg, but he cannot talk or eat. His doctor said that about a third of his brain is damaged already. It has been over three weeks, and the doctor said they do not see any improvement. He is sixty-three years old. My question is, will he recover from this?
text: My dad had a basal ganglia stroke on August twenty eighth, two thousand and twelve. He can move his right arm and leg, but he cannot talk or eat. His doctor said that about a third of his brain is damaged already. It has been over three weeks, and the doctor said they did not see any improvement. He is sixty three years old. The question is, we recover from it.
